mnemonics.title: Black Box Testing
mnemonics.content: Black Box testing is like checking a wrapped gift box. You don't know what's inside, but you can check the outside to make sure it's what you expect.

mnemonics.title: White Box Testing
mnemonics.content: White Box testing is like checking the inside of a clock. You need to know how the clock works to make sure it's working correctly.

acronyms.usage: BBT is used to test the external behavior of the software.
WBT is used to test the internal structure and logic of the software.

visualTips.visualization: Imagine dividing a pizza into slices based on toppings. Each slice represents a partition of the input data.
Imagine a number line with boundaries marked. Each boundary represents a point where the software's behavior changes.

analogies.analogy: Imagine a vending machine that dispenses snacks based on the state of the machine. Each state represents a different outcome.
Imagine a map with multiple paths to a destination. Each path represents a different sequence of events.
